message.content: {"schema_version":"stella.literature_hvs_candidates.v0.1","generated_at":"2026-06-13T11:24:54","paper":{"arxiv_id":"1901.04559","bibcode":"2019ApJ...872...58H","title":"Identifying Sagittarius Stream Stars By Their APOGEE Chemical Abundance Signatures","month":"2019-01","source_note_json":"notes/2019/2019-01/2019-01.json","links":{"abs":"https://arxiv.org/abs/1901.04559","pdf":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/1901.04559"}},"inputs":{"paper_dir":"literature/1901.04559","audit_path":"literature/1901.04559/audit.json","catalog_review_path":"literature/1901.04559/catalog_review.json","catalog_extraction_path":"literature/1901.04559/catalog_extraction.json","ecsv_paths":[]},"extraction":{"status":"candidates_found","extracted_at":"2026-06-13T11:24:54","extractor":"agent","summary":"One HVS candidate identified. At ~54 kpc Galactocentric distance with total velocity ~577^{+209}_{-129} km/s, the star is completely unbound from the Milky Way in MWPotential2014. Classified as early M-giant. Full Table 1 with APOGEE IDs and all 68 Sgr chemical candidates is not fully accessible from the provided sources; only the HVS candidate (membership class 'd') is individually identifiable from the paper text.","tooling":{"agent_runtime":"","model_id":"","prompt_version":"","request_parameters":{}}},"method_chain":[{"id":"step-01","depends_on":[],"step_type":"input_catalog","summary":"APOGEE DR14 survey. APOGEE-1 observed 146,000 stars; DR14 adds ~100,000 more in the Northern Hemisphere. High-resolution (R~22,500) near-infrared (1.51-1.70 μm) spectrograph on the Sloan 2.5m telescope. Stellar parameters and chemical abundances from ASPCAP pipeline.","inputs":["APOGEE DR14"],"outputs":["spectra","stellar_parameters","chemical_abundances","radial_velocities"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":159,"end_line":164,"context":"APOGEE survey description, DR14, ASPCAP pipeline"}]},{"id":"step-02","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"cross_match","summary":"Cross-match APOGEE DR14 stars with Gaia DR2 proper motions using nearest-neighbor matching within 1 arcsec via TOPCAT. 62 of 68 chemical candidates have Gaia proper motion matches.","inputs":["APOGEE DR14 catalog","Gaia DR2"],"outputs":["matched_catalog_with_proper_motions"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":177,"end_line":179,"context":"Gaia DR2 cross-match with APOGEE using TOPCAT nearest-neighbor within 1 arcsec"}]},{"id":"step-03","depends_on":["step-02"],"step_type":"quality_filter","summary":"Select APOGEE stars with 3550<Teff<4200 K, S/N>70 per half resolution element, no ASPCAPBAD flag, and [Fe/H]>-1.2. These criteria match the Sgr core sample parameters.","inputs":["matched_catalog"],"outputs":["quality_filtered_sample"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":165,"end_line":173,"context":"Quality cuts: Teff 3550-4200 K, S/N>70, no ASPCAPBAD, [Fe/H]>-1.2"}]},{"id":"step-04","depends_on":["step-03"],"step_type":"distance_estimation","summary":"Spectro-photometric distances from the DR14 value-added catalog (NMSU_dist estimator from Hayden et al. 2014), accurate to ~20%. Adopted over Gaia parallax distances for homogeneity across the >5 kpc distance range.","inputs":["quality_filtered_sample"],"outputs":["distances"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":175,"end_line":175,"context":"Spectro-photometric distances from DR14 VAC, ~20% accuracy"}]},{"id":"step-05","depends_on":["step-01"],"step_type":"radial_velocity_measurement","summary":"Radial velocities measured by the APOGEE pipeline.","inputs":["APOGEE spectra"],"outputs":["radial_velocities"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":161,"end_line":164,"context":"APOGEE pipeline processes spectra to derive stellar parameters and RVs"}]},{"id":"step-06","depends_on":["step-03"],"step_type":"sample_selection","summary":"K-means clustering on 6D chemical abundance space [(C+N)/Fe, O/Fe, Mg/Fe, Al/Fe, Mn/Fe, Ni/Fe] to identify 68 Sgr chemical candidates. k=16 chosen so that 10% of Sgr core sample separates from the Sgr cluster.","inputs":["quality_filtered_sample","chemical_abundances"],"outputs":["68_Sgr_chemical_candidates"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":218,"end_line":242,"context":"K-means clustering on 6D chemical space with k=16"}]},{"id":"step-07","depends_on":[],"step_type":"solar_position_and_motion","summary":"Adopt R0=8.0 kpc, Vcirc=220 km/s at the Solar radius, and solar peculiar motion from Schonrich et al. (2010). Left-handed Galactic Cartesian coordinate system with X(U) positive toward Galactic center, Y(V) along rotation, Z(W) toward north Galactic pole.","inputs":[],"outputs":["solar_parameters"],"parameters":[{"name":"R0","raw_value":"8.0 kpc","value":"8.0","unit":"kpc","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Solar radius of 8.0 kpc"}]},{"name":"v_circ_sun","raw_value":"220 km s^{-1}","value":"220","unit":"km/s","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Circular velocity of 220 km/s at Solar radius"}]},{"name":"solar_motion_u","raw_value":"Solar motion as measured by \\citet{Schoenrich2010}","value":"not_reported","unit":"","description":"Individual U component value not stated; paper references Schonrich et al. (2010)","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Solar motion from Schonrich et al. 2010, individual U value not stated"}]},{"name":"solar_motion_v","raw_value":"Solar motion as measured by \\citet{Schoenrich2010}","value":"not_reported","unit":"","description":"Individual V component value not stated; paper references Schonrich et al. (2010)","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Solar motion from Schonrich et al. 2010, individual V value not stated"}]},{"name":"solar_motion_w","raw_value":"Solar motion as measured by \\citet{Schoenrich2010}","value":"not_reported","unit":"","description":"Individual W component value not stated; paper references Schonrich et al. (2010)","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":181,"context":"Solar motion from Schonrich et al. 2010, individual W value not stated"}]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Solar parameters and coordinate system assumptions"}]},{"id":"step-08","depends_on":[],"step_type":"galactic_potential_model","summary":"MWPotential2014 from Bovy (2015) galpy: composite of spheroidal bulge, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, and NFW halo. Authors note halo mass may be too low but relative orbital properties are the focus.","inputs":[],"outputs":["MWPotential2014_potential"],"parameters":[{"name":"potential_name","raw_value":"MWPotential2014","value":"MWPotential2014","description":"Composite of spheroidal bulge, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, and NFW halo in galpy","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"MWPotential2014 potential description"}]}],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Galactic potential model"}]},{"id":"step-09","depends_on":["step-04","step-05","step-02","step-07"],"step_type":"velocity_calculation","summary":"Calculate full 6D phase-space velocities (UVW) and total Galactocentric velocity using Gaia DR2 proper motions, APOGEE distances, and radial velocities, transformed to Galactocentric frame using solar parameters from step-07.","inputs":["distances","radial_velocities","proper_motions","solar_parameters"],"outputs":["UVW_velocities","total_galactocentric_velocity"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Velocity calculation using proper motions, distances, RVs, and solar parameters"}]},{"id":"step-10","depends_on":["step-09","step-08"],"step_type":"orbit_integration","summary":"Integrate orbits forward and backward for 1.5 Gyr in MWPotential2014 using galpy. Initialize at each star's current position and velocity. Estimate orbital parameters.","inputs":["galactocentric_velocities","MWPotential2014_potential"],"outputs":["orbital_parameters"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Orbit integration in MWPotential2014 for 1.5 Gyr forward and backward"}]},{"id":"step-11","depends_on":["step-10"],"step_type":"escape_or_bound_assessment","summary":"Determine bound/unbound status from integrated orbits. The HVS candidate's observed velocities suggest it is completely unbound from the Milky Way in MWPotential2014 (no measured apocenter, ~760 kpc at last timestep). Orbits for min/max space velocities within uncertainties are also unbound. This step assesses bound status only.","inputs":["orbital_parameters"],"outputs":["bound_status"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":296,"end_line":296,"context":"Star is unbound in MWPotential2014, no measured apocenter (~760 kpc at last timestep)"},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":402,"end_line":402,"context":"Orbits for min/max space velocities within uncertainties are also unbound"}]},{"id":"step-12","depends_on":["step-11","step-09"],"step_type":"origin_assessment","summary":"The HVS candidate is an early M-giant with v_tot~577 km/s at ~54 kpc Galactocentric distance. Orbit does not trace back to Galactic center, LMC, or M31. Binary runaway unlikely for M-giant. Dynamical interaction (triple system or binary+massive star) considered but unlikely given old metal-poor environment. Possible systematic error in Gaia proper motions noted.","inputs":["bound_status","orbital_parameters","stellar_type"],"outputs":["HVS_candidate_classification","origin_discussion"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":388,"end_line":403,"context":"HVS candidate origin discussion: early M-giant, orbit doesn't trace back to GC, LMC, or M31, systematic PM error possible"}]},{"id":"step-13","depends_on":["step-10"],"step_type":"other","summary":"Extract specific orbital parameters (eccentricity, peri- and apo-center distances, orbital energy, angular momentum) from the integrated orbit results for classification into orbital groups (Sgr stream, accreted halo, bulge, HVS, others).","inputs":["orbital_parameters"],"outputs":["eccentricity","r_peri","r_apo","orbital_energy","angular_momentum"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":181,"end_line":182,"context":"Orbital parameter estimation: eccentricity, peri/apo-center, energy, angular momentum"},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":290,"end_line":298,"context":"Classification of candidates into orbital groups based on eccentricity and r_apo"}]},{"id":"step-14","depends_on":["step-11"],"step_type":"other","summary":"Origin assessment metrics. For the HVS candidate, total Galactocentric velocity ~577^{+209}_{-129} km/s, closest approach r_peri~53 kpc in ~20 Myr. Orbit passes nowhere near LMC or M31. Escape velocity from MWPotential2014 not explicitly stated; the star is simply assessed as unbound.","inputs":["bound_status","orbital_parameters"],"outputs":["origin_metrics"],"parameters":[],"source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":388,"end_line":403,"context":"Origin assessment: HVS candidate origin discussion and escape velocity context"}]}],"candidates":[{"identifiers":{"record_id":"1901.04559:cand-001","paper_candidate_id":"HVS candidate","gaia_source_id":"","all":[{"value":"HVS candidate","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":291,"end_line":296,"context":"HVS (1 star) classification in orbital groups"}]},{"value":"membership class d","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":321,"end_line":321,"context":"Table 1 footnote: d = HVS"}]}]}}],"candidate_groups_considered":[{"group_id":"table-1-stream-candidates","description":"Full Sgr stream cluster from Table 1. Contains 68 stars: 35 Sgr stream (a), 20 accreted halo (b), 4 bulge (c), 1 HVS (d), 2 others (e). Only the HVS is possibly unbound.","decision":"excluded","reason":"Of 68 candidates, 35 are Sgr stream members on bound tidal debris orbits, 20 are accreted halo stars on bound eccentric orbits, 4 are bulge stars, and 2 are unclassified others. Only the 1 HVS candidate is unbound. Full Table 1 is available only from the online journal and not in the provided sources.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":291,"end_line":298,"context":"Orbital groups: Sgr Stream (35), Accreted Halo (20), Bulge (4), HVS (1), Others (2)"},{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":304,"end_line":325,"context":"Table 1 header, partial entries, and footnote defining membership classes a-e"}]},{"group_id":"accreted-halo-group","description":"20 accreted halo stars with high eccentricities (e>0.75) and median r_apo=20.2 kpc, possibly from ancient major merger 8-11 Gyr ago. Sgr-like chemistry but bound orbits.","decision":"excluded","reason":"These stars are on bound orbits. The paper describes them as accreted halo stars, not as unbound/HVS candidates.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":356,"end_line":361,"context":"Accreted halo stars: high eccentricities, median r_apo=20.2 kpc, bound orbits"}]},{"group_id":"sgr-stream-group","description":"35 Sgr stream members consistent with Law and Majewski (2010) model, stripped during last two pericenter passages.","decision":"excluded","reason":"These stars are Sgr tidal debris members on bound orbits within the Milky Way potential, not unbound/HVS candidates.","source_refs":[{"kind":"text","path":"literature/1901.04559/arxiv_source/trace_sgr_stream_resub.tex","start_line":338,"end_line":344,"context":"35 Sgr stream stars with positions and kinematics consistent with LM10 model, bound tidal debris"}]}]}
message.reasoning_content:
Let me analyze the validation errors:

1. `$.method_chain[6].parameters[2].value` - contains LaTeX residue "not_stated" - wait, the issue is that the raw_value contains "Schonrich et al. (2010)" and the value is "not_stated". The problem is that the value should be a cleaned machine value. Looking at the schema, these are solar motion parameters (u, v, w). The paper doesn't state the individual U/V/W values, just references Schonrich et al. (2010). The raw_value has the citation text, and value should be something like "not_reported" or similar. But the error says "contains LaTeX residue". Let me re-read...


Actually, the raw_value for the solar motion parameters includes "Schonrich et al. (2010)" - the "ö" in "Schonrich" is written as a LaTeX escape, which is LaTeX residue. But the value field says "not_stated" which shouldn't have LaTeX residue. Let me re-read the error message: "contains LaTeX residue; keep it in raw_value and store a cleaned machine value here". The raw_value should keep the LaTeX, but the value should be cleaned. Since the paper doesn't state individual U/V/W values, the value should be something like "not_reported" - but the error says the value contains LaTeX residue, so I need to check what's actually in the value field and clean it properly.

Looking at the specific error about `$.method_chain[6].parameters[2].value`, which corresponds to step-06's third parameter (index 2), I'm realizing the parameter list might be different than I thought. Step-06 has the sample selection summary, so I need to trace through which parameter is actually being flagged and ensure the value field is free of any LaTeX formatting. The value fields I stored as "not_stated" shouldn't trigger the LaTeX residue error, so maybe the validator is flagging something else about these method parameter records. Let me reconsider whether "not_stated" is actually the appropriate value format for these fields.

For the solar motion parameters, since the paper references Schonrich et al. (2010) without providing explicit numerical values, I should either extract those known values from the referenced paper or mark them as "not_reported" instead of "not_stated". The cleanest approach is to set the value to "not_reported" while keeping the raw citation in the raw_value field.

Now I'm looking at the second set of errors in the candidates section—the validation is flagging that method step 'step-09' is being used as a direct producer for incompatible quantity categories.  The issue is that I have step-09 (velocity_calculation) being referenced for both orbit and velocity fields, but these need to be split into separate steps since they're distinct categories in the method chain. Looking at the schema requirements, I see the direct producer rules map velocity fields to `velocity_calculation` and orbit parameters to `orbit_integration` — so step-09 handles total velocity while step-10 handles the orbit. The validator seems to expect distinct steps for these different quantity types rather than reusing a single step across them. I need to split step-09 into two separate steps: one for the velocity calculation itself and another for the orbit parameters that depend on it. I also need to create a separate escape assessment step for the bound probability since the validator is flagging incompatible category assignments. Let me restructure the method chain so each step handles only compatible quantity categories. I need to restructure step-09 to only calculate velocities without orbit parameters, then let step-10 handle the orbit integration. For step-11, I should split out the bound/unbound assessment from any quality or probability metrics to avoid category conflicts.

I'm also noticing the validator flagged LaTeX residue in some method parameters—likely from the citation text in the raw values. I'll replace those with "not_reported" to keep things clean, then focus on the structural fixes that are actually blocking validation.

The real issues are step-09 and step-11: step-09 should be renamed to something like "orbital_parameter_estimation" to properly reflect what it produces, and step-11 needs to be split so it doesn't create a category mismatch between bound_assessment and quality fields. I'll create a new step-13 to separate the bound status assessment from the quality-related aspects, then update the method chain accordingly. I'm reconsidering whether the validator is actually looking at method step types or quantity categories instead. Looking at the QuantityRecord schema, it doesn't include a category field, so maybe the issue is about how step-09 and step-11 are being used across different candidate field types like bound_assessment, velocity, and orbit. Let me reconsider what the validator is really checking for when it flags step-09 for incompatible categories.

I'm going to split step-09 into two separate steps: keeping it for velocity calculations like tangential and total velocity, and creating a new step-13 specifically for orbit parameters like eccentricity, pericenter, apocenter, and angular momentum. I also need to fix step-11 to handle bound/unbound probability separately from the escape velocity assessment.

For step-06's parameters with "not_stated" values, I'm checking whether the validator expects "not_reported" as the canonical form, and I notice there might be LaTeX formatting issues in the raw values that need cleaning up. I'm realizing that "not_stated" is actually a valid string and doesn't contain LaTeX formatting, so the validator message might be misleading. The real issue could be that method parameters shouldn't use quantity-like fields at all. Let me adjust these to use "not_reported" instead and remove any extra fields that might be triggering the validation, then refocus on the main structural problems with the method chain itself.

For the step-09 conflict between orbit and velocity categories, I'll keep it focused only on velocity calculations and create a separate step-13 for orbital parameters. For step-11's mixing of bound assessment and quality fields, I'll assign it to bound assessment only and add a distinct step-14 for quality-related metrics. Since this is the scaffold stage with just identifier stubs, these category issues must be preemptive validation based on how I've described the steps, so I need to be more careful about what each step is meant to handle.

Looking at the direct producer rules, velocity calculations map to step-09, orbit parameters to step-10, and escape assessments to step-11. I realize I'm trying to use step-10 for both orbit integration and orbital parameter estimation, which is causing the overlap. I should split these into separate steps or clarify that step-10 handles orbit integration while step-13 covers the orbital parameter estimation aspect.

Wait, I'm noticing a potential circular dependency issue: if step-13 depends on step-10, and step-10 depends on step-13, that creates a cycle. Let me trace through this more carefully—step-10 does orbit integration and produces orbital parameters, then step-13 does orbital parameter estimation which depends on step-10, but step-10 also depends on step-09 for velocity calculation, so the dependency chain should actually be step-09 → step-10 → step-13, not circular. Step-11 uses the orbital parameters from step-10 to determine the bound status, and step-12 takes that assessment along with the velocity information to make origin determinations. So the flow is step-09 and step-10 feeding into step-11, which then combines with step-09 results for step-12. Now I'm adding a couple of additional steps: step-13 for orbital parameter estimation that depends on step-10, and step-14 for origin assessment tied to step-11. Then I'll update the step-06 parameters to use "not_reported" instead of "not_stated", and make sure the LaTeX formatting in raw_value is properly handled while the value field stays clean.

Looking at the validation errors more carefully, they're all pointing to LaTeX residue in the value fields—specifically the citation text in step-06 parameters 2-4. The raw_value should preserve that citation, but the value needs to be stripped down to just "not_reported" without the formatted citation. My plan is to leave raw_value as-is with the citation text intact, then set value to "not_reported" for those three parameters, split step-09 to remove orbit-dependent outputs, and handle the step-11 category issue next.

For step-11, I need to clarify what it actually represents—the error mentions 'quality' as a category, which doesn't align with bound_assessment. I'm wondering if the validator is inferring categories from the step type itself, or if there's something in the outputs that's triggering this mismatch. Let me think through what categories should actually be assigned to escape_or_bound_assessment versus what's currently there.

Looking at the Category enum, there's no explicit 'quality' option, so I'm going to try a different strategy: keep step-11 tightly focused on just the bound/unbound assessment with outputs like bound_status or unbound_probability, and create a separate step-14 to handle any origin or hypothesis metrics that might be bleeding into the wrong category. This should resolve the validator complaint about mixing bound_assessment with other incompatible categories.

For step-09, I'm realizing the validator error about 'orbit' and 'velocity' might stem from how I've classified the outputs—velocity and orbit are actually separate fields in the schema under ObservedPhaseSpace versus OrbitRecord, so I need to make sure they're not conflated in the same step.

Now I'm going through the solar motion parameters and cleaning up the LaTeX formatting in the raw values. For each of the U, V, and W components, I'm setting the value to "not_reported" to indicate the paper doesn't provide these values explicitly. I'm also restructuring the method chain to properly separate velocity calculations from orbital parameters, and clarifying that step-11 focuses exclusively on the bound assessment without mixing in other assessment types. Still writing the JSON structure...
